mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces positive returns in less than 52% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 8%, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising federal funds rates and falling industrial production indicate a monetary policy overtightening into a contracting real economy, jointly amplifying discount rate pressure and earnings risk to drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS changes and INDPRO changes exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint condition fails to produce negative returns in more than 45% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in housing starts during post-recession recovery phases signals durable goods demand and construction employment multiplier effects, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods classified as post-recession recovery, month-over-month increases in HOUST do not coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or the mean excess return is not positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates an immediate contraction in manufacturing and utilities output, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: Triggered instances of sharp INDPRO declines produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of occurrences, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a self-reinforcing expansion in labor income and consumer expenditures, amplifying corporate revenue growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth signals an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand and corporate revenues, leading to broad-based equity risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of negative GDPC1 growth are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unusually large month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals accelerating inflation that compresses real corporate margins and forces tighter future monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL changes above 0.4% fail to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).
